text:
Market today: Mixed mood with a hawkish undercurrent. Cleveland Fed's Hammack floating rate HIKES is the real driver - that's the single biggest threat to your leveraged, margin-heavy tech book. AI inflation fears compress multiples fast.

Your holdings: PTNM is down -15.25% and bleeding - tiny spec on margin is the most dangerous spot right now, stop at $9.50 is close, watch it hard. SOXL at +3.47% sounds fine but it's 3x leveraged and decays - any semiconductor selloff hits you triple; stop $200 feels wide. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - margin amplifies any gap-down badly.

Watch: 1) Fed rate-hike language - any escalation kills chip/growth names fast. 2) Semiconductor sentiment via SMH - if it cracks, SOXL and your whole book follow.

Bottom line: Protect capital first - tighten SOXL and cut PTNM if it approaches $9.50, because margin turns small mistakes into big damage.
plays: PTNM: Stop is at $9.50 - if price touches it, exit without hesitation. Down -15.25% on a tiny spec on margin is already painful; no reason to let it get worse.
SOXL: Consider tightening your stop from $200 toward $220-230. 3x leverage plus hawkish Fed talk is a bad combo; the wide stop means a massive dollar loss if chips roll over.
No new buys today - rate-hike headlines and a margin-heavy book say protect, not add.
